mappings.rationale: Directly covers galaxy classification, physical properties, clusters, radio and X-ray galaxies, quasars, and the Milky Way central to this section.
Directly covers stellar characteristics, the Hertzsprung-Russell diagram, stellar evolution, variable stars, and stellar structure discussed in this section.
Covers the physics of galaxies and stars including stellar structure, stellar atmospheres, and galactic dynamics discussed throughout this section.
Relevant to the large-scale distribution of galaxies, galaxy clusters, quasars, and the evolution of galaxies discussed in this section.
Relevant to quasars, active galactic nuclei, extragalactic X-ray sources, and the end states of stellar evolution discussed in this section.
Relevant to stellar properties and structure, as the Sun is the nearest star and provides the basis for understanding stellar characteristics.
Covers the historical development of our understanding of galaxies, stellar classification, and the structure of the Milky Way.
Relevant to thermonuclear reactions in stars that power stellar energy generation and produce chemical elements discussed in this section.
Covers the observational instruments used to determine distances, luminosities, spectra, and other stellar and galactic properties.
Relevant to stellar spectra, the electromagnetic spectrum, and the observational methods for determining stellar characteristics.
Covers the fundamental physical processes including nuclear fusion, radiation, and gravity that govern stellar and galactic behaviour.
Relevant to gravitational dynamics of galaxies, stellar formation through gravitational contraction, and galactic rotation discussed in this section.
Relevant to the physics of compact stellar remnants, relativistic effects in extreme gravitational fields, and galactic dynamics.
Relevant to nuclear processes in stars and the formation of radioactive elements through stellar nucleosynthesis.
Relevant to interstellar magnetic fields, the magnetic field of the Galaxy, and their effects on cosmic rays and radio waves.
Relevant to the formation of planetary systems around stars as part of the broader stellar evolution discussed in this section.
Relevant to the formation of chemical elements in stars through nucleosynthesis, a key topic in stellar evolution.
Relevant to stellar nucleosynthesis and the formation of isotopes in stars, connecting to chemical evolution of the Galaxy.
Relevant to the historical development of stellar nomenclature, celestial coordinate systems, and constellations discussed in this section.
